rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible AADL features and declared helpers in AGREE annexes; avoid referencing implementation-only paths in type-level contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE annexes must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Generated AGREE code must reference only identifiers and types present in the analyzed AADL model.
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence, and branching logic should use nested if-then-else expressions.
Component type contracts should relate visible inputs to visible outputs without relying on internal subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with proper AGREE logical operators and syntax.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; only visible ports and type-level helpers are allowed.
Avoid referencing AADL property names or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst tasks such as AADL structural extraction
